Design philosophy

The interface is the decision.

When you design for operators in power plants, you learn that every pixel has a cost. I carry that standard into everything I build.

01

Clarity over cleverness

A beautiful interface that confuses the user has failed. Every decision starts with one question: does this help the user act with confidence?

02

Research is not optional

The problem you think exists is rarely the problem that matters. I talk to actual users — not personas, not assumptions — before designing anything.

03

Design closes the loop

I write HTML, CSS, and React. I review implementations. The gap between design intent and built reality is where most UX value is lost — I refuse to let that happen.

The challenge

ABB's System 800xA had accumulated 15 years of legacy decisions — dense data layouts, inconsistent workflows, and a growing gap between what the system could tell operators and what they could confidently act on.

Context & brief

ABB Ability System 800xA is used in power plants, oil & gas facilities, pharmaceutical manufacturing, and industrial environments globally. The platform had evolved over 15 years, accumulating design debt and an emerging need to integrate AI-driven insights without disrupting expert operator workflows.

My role

  • UX research & stakeholder interviews
  • Heuristic evaluation & cognitive load mapping
  • Human + AI Collaboration Model definition
  • User flows, wireframes & high-fidelity prototypes
  • Design system contribution (APUX & Arcadia)
  • Sprint facilitation & mentoring 6 junior designers
Research & discovery
01
🎙️

Stakeholder Interviews

Operators, engineers, and domain experts across global sites — mapping real decision patterns and frustrations.

02
🔬

Heuristic Evaluation

Nielsen's 10 heuristics across 40+ screens — 87 usability issues ranked by severity and frequency.

03
🧠

Cognitive Load Mapping

Information density, attention paths, and decision bottlenecks in dashboards under time pressure.

04
📊

Workflow Analysis

12 critical operator workflows — identifying where AI insights surfaced without context, causing hesitation.

Core design work

The Human + AI
Collaboration Model

AI predictions were already in the platform — but operators were ignoring them. Trust wasn't failing because the AI was wrong. It was failing because the interface didn't communicate why or how confident it was.

👁️
Mode 01 — Passive

Background Analyst

AI monitors continuously, surfaces anomalies passively. Operators gain awareness without distraction.

🔔
Mode 02 — Active

Alert System

Contextual alerts with confidence levels and supporting data. Engineered to cut alarm fatigue dramatically.

🤝
Mode 03 — Collaborative

Recommendation Engine

AI proposes actions with rationale. The operator reviews, approves, or overrides — always the final decision-maker.

Design & delivery

ABB Ability System 800xA — POC Workflow Editor and industrial control interface

Design & delivery

  • Progressive disclosure — context-relevant information only
  • Contextual AI panels — inline, dismissible, confidence indicators
  • Redesigned alarm management — priority-ranked with history
  • Batch workflow simplification — 12-step process reduced to 5

Collaboration

  • Weekly design scrums with engineering & product
  • Bi-weekly usability reviews with domain experts
  • Specs, rationale & decision documentation
  • Reusable pattern library in APUX & Arcadia
  • Mentored 6 junior UX designers

"The goal wasn't to make the system look modern. It was to make the operator's decision in the next 30 seconds feel obvious — even when the data is anything but."

— Thomas Ravi Kiran
Outcomes & impact
25–35%
Faster operator decision-making
Validated through comparative usability testing. Operators reached correct decisions faster with lower error rates.
15 yrs
Legacy complexity resolved
Mapped and addressed 15 years of accumulated design debt from IA through interaction patterns.
87
Usability issues identified
Heuristic evaluation across 40+ screens — prioritised by severity, creating a clear design backlog.
3
AI interaction modes defined
Background Analyst, Alert System, and Recommendation Engine — each with distinct visual language.

What this taught me

Industrial UX demands a different muscle than consumer product design. The user is an expert. The consequences of a wrong decision can be catastrophic. The interface must amplify judgment, not replace it.

The biggest lesson: AI features fail not because the model is wrong, but because the interface doesn't give operators the confidence to act on it. Trust is a design problem.

What I believe

Three principles that shape every decision.

After 7+ years designing systems that real people depend on, these aren't abstract values — they're lessons earned in difficult projects.

01

Clarity over cleverness

A beautiful interface that confuses the user has failed. Every decision starts with: does this help the user act with confidence? If not, I simplify.

02

Design closes the loop

I write HTML, CSS, and React. I review implementations. The gap between design intent and built reality is where most UX value is lost.

03

Research is not optional

Every design that had real impact started with talking to actual users. Not personas. Not assumptions. The problem you think exists is rarely the real problem.
